title: Croft's Rowe's Wharf Observation
description: Elias Croft, visibly relieved to be treated as a witness rather than a suspect, tells you what he saw two nights ago at Rowe's Wharf: Dillworth meeting a man in a dark coat who handed him something small and flat — the size of a folded banknote or a receipt. Croft adds, almost as an afterthought, that he cannot read or write, which means he could not have authored any coded correspondence even if he wanted to.
